notes: In Kafr al-Sheikh, Nile Delta governorate, hundreds of Muslim Brotherhood members gathered carrying sticks, marching through the streets
In al-Massala Sqaure, dozens of opponents protested outside the FJP headquarters
Dozens of members of the Muslim Brotherhood besieged the Kerdasa police stationin Giza, expressing anger at thepolice for joining the anti-Morsy protesters and for failing to establish security

Protesters continued their sit-in for a third day outside Monufiyas local governorate office on Wednesday, demonstrating against the appointment of Muslim Brotherhood member Ahmed Sharawy as the new governor
In a separate incident, Kafr al-Sheikh Governor and Muslim Brotherhood leader Saad al-Husseiny accused members of the Tamarod campaign of setting his car on fire and assaulting his home in Mahalla
In Tanta in Gharbiya, hundreds blocked the seaside road to protest the appointment of Brotherhood leader Ahmed al-Bialy as governor.
Opposition forces in Beheira protested to reject their new governor
In Ismailia, activists also staged mass protests outside the governorate building to express their anger at perceived Brotherhood affiliate, Hassan al-Hawy, taking the position of governor.
In Monufiya, dozens of activists demonstrated outside the governorate building in Shebin al-Kom, hours after the news that Ahmed Shaarawy, a Brotherhood leader, would become governor.
Muslim Brotherhood members expressed their admiration and support for President Mohammed Morsy during protests at Amr Ebn al-Aas Mosque in Giza
Thousands of demonstrators on Friday gathered at Damiettas al-Saaa Square to protest against the rule of President Mohamed Mursi and the Muslim Brotherhood.
Protesters in Monofeyas Shebin al-Kom on Friday participated in marches to demand holding early presidential elections and amending disputed articles in the constitution.
Several parties on Friday organised protests in Suezs al-Arbaeen Square to demand ousting President Mohamed Mursi and sacking Prime Minister Hisham Kandil and Interior Minister Mohamed Ibrahim

notes: Security forces arrested 13 demonstrators engaged in a peaceful sit-in protest at the home of the prime minister in Dokki, calling for his resignation.
Policemen held a protest in front of North Sinais Arish Security Directorate, saying that they have been left without protection to face militants attacks.
Dozens scuffled in Ismailiya, east of Cairo after an anti-Morsi protest
Scores of demonstrators on Saturday protested against President Mohamed Mursi and the Muslim Brotherhood

Egyptian security forces and military police chased and arrested 6 activists in the city of Suez after a court jailed 7 rights activists for between 6 months and three years. Nine people were reportedly injured in the attack.

Hundreds marched from Alexandrias al-Qaed Ibrahim mosque headed to the Northern military area in Sidi Gaber to call for full transfer of power and denounce the complementary constitutional declaration issued by the ruling Supreme Council of Armed Forces (SCAF).
Amidst protests in Cairo, Alexandria and Suez, In Damietta, hundreds organised a march where a number of revolutionaries and Salafists participated.
hundreds marched in Al-Shatbi area downtown protesting the election outcome
Sudanese citizens and Darfuri refugees rallied in front of the UN High Commissioner for refugees in Cairo

notes: About 25 people protested outside the HQ of the al-Fagr newspaper in Dokki district. The protesters were angry because the newspaper was seen as anti-military.
About 20 people protested in Luxor over a land dispute. There were reports that the protesters had attacked tourists but the Egyptian authorities later claimed no violence occurred.
28 detainees broke out of Al-Montazah Prison after they set their detention room on fire. 7 were later re-arrested.
Drivers demonstrated in Kafr el-Sheikh to protest against a fuel shortage.
Workers of Nile Cotton Ginning Company protested and staged a sit-in in Mahalla el-Kobra.
Hundreds of Copts in Alexandria demonstrated against the recent violence in Imbaba.
Taxi drivers protested in Beni Sueif governorate to demand a rise in fares.
Hundreds of Bedouin blocked roads in South Sinai governorateto demand the release of their relatives.

Christian and Muslim protesters demonstrated for a 2nd day outside the Bibliotheca Alexandrina to demand unity between Muslims and Copts and to denounce the alleged reluctance of the SCAF to fight sectarianism.
More than 2,000 workers of a private ceramic tile factory in el-Sadat City went on strike and staged 2 days of demonstrations to demand bonuses and permanent employment. They are members of the ETUF.

Student protests demanding the resignations of senior university figures were successful in Helwan, Zagazig, Alexandria, Asyut and Sohag.

About 3,000 fishermen protested outside the coastguard offices in Kafr el-Sheikh to demand enroachments on el-Borollous Lake be removed and more licenses for fish farms be created.

After a bloggers case was deferred until next month (or potentially indefinitely), tens of demonstrators started a protest outside the court against the decision. The protesters accused the Ministry of Justice of bias in the case.

Thousands of Salafist presidential candidate Hazem Salah Abu Ismail supporters demonstrated in Cairos Tahrir square while hundreds demonstrated in Alexandria to show their support for him in light of the ongoing debate on his mothers reported US citizenship.
Workers protested in front of a Telemisr factory on Al Haram street after the owners shut down the factory .
Nearly 2,000 pro-Palestinian protesters gathered outside the Israeli embassy in the Oula area of Giza as Israeli warplanes and tanks carried out attacks neighboring Gaza.
Some residents of Abu Qurqas staged a protest on the Cairo-Aswan road in their area against recent violent clashes between Muslims and Copts in their area.
Protests erupted in Suez after the current governor of the area honored the former governor who has been accused of being responsible for the deaths of anti-government protesters and corruption.

About 5,000 residents of Suez governorate held a symbolic popular trial for their former governor outside the El-Arbeen Mosque in which he was accused of being responsible for killing anti-government protesters in the area and corruption. Their verdict was to set the former governor on fire as an example to other governors.

A few hundred protesters rallied in front of the Qena governorates HQ following calls for a million man march to reiterate demands to sack the current governor, after PM Essam Sharaf suspended him for 3 months on Apr.25. The Coalition of the January 25 Youth were one of the groups which attended the protest.
Protesters in Qina stormed a press conference being held by the Egyptian PM. Some were chanting that they want a Muslim governor for the province.
Over 300 protesters marched from the Qaed Ibrahim Mosque to the Israeli Embassy in Kafr Abdo district to show solidarity with Palestinians.
Christian and Muslim protesters demonstrated outside the Bibliotheca Alexandrina to demand unity between Muslims and Copts and to denounce the alleged reluctance of the SCAF to fight sectarianism.
Protesters, waving Palestinian flags, protest outside the Israeli Embassy. The military fires warning shots to disperse the protesters.
A number of activists heading for Gaza which managed to make it past military checkpoints set up across the Sinai Peninsula protested at the Rafah border crossing in solidarity with Palestinians.
Hundreds of people protested outside the hospital where former President Mubarak is said to be staying in Sharm el-Sheikh.

5 protests were held in different cities in South Sinai. Bedouins blocked major roads, set tires on fire, and marched with guns. They were protesting against rumours of a potential amnesty deal for Mubarak and against the negative effects on tourism in the area.
Hundreds of citizens protested in Al-Arish to demand a quicker trial for former President Hosni Mubarak.
Thousands protested in front of the Al-Qaed Ibrahim Mosque in Alexandria for a quicker trial for former President Hosni Mubarak.
A few thousand people protested on El-Arbiein street in Suez to demand a quicker trial for former President Hosni Mubarak.

Protests to keep up pressure on the SCAF to prosecute corruption and continue reform of the government were reported in Ismailia, Fayoum and Aswan.

Thousands of protesters demonstrated at El-Arbaein Square in Suez as part of ongoing demonstrations in major Egyptian cities.
Protesters claim four individuals who had started a hunger strike were tortured by police in Suez.
Protests were again held at Al-Arbaein Square in Suez to demand faster reform of the government by the SCAF.
Dozens of Egyptians angered by the absence of police protection in their town set fire to a police station after looting its armory and freeing jailed inmates.
Protests were staged in Suez alongside large protests in Tahrir Square against the SCAFs new electoral law and to demand the end of the state of emergency.
Students of the MSA started a sit-in protest and strike to demand better quality of education, facilities and activities.
Protesting MSA students engaged in a sit-in since Monday were locked in campus and not allowed to leave by university security on Thursday night.
Clashes have broken out near the security directorate office in the Smouha area of Alexandria every night between Nov.19-22.

Students at Cairo Universitys Faculty of Economics and Political Science protested on campus in solidarity with the victims of the Qasr al-Aini street clashes.

notes: Hundreds of protesters marched to condemn attacks against female protesters and demand a transition from military rule in Asyut. A number of organizations participated, including the Revolutionary Youth Coalition and the SDP and DFP.
Demos against military rule were held in Port Said, Ismailia, and Luxor.
Demos against military rule were held in Port Said, Ismailia, and Luxor.

Hundreds of protesters marched on Arbain Square in Suez to protest against the SCAF and recent attacks on female demonstrators. The demo was organized by the Suez Youth Bloc.
A small group of protesters demanded an end to military rule in Tanta.
About 500 people gathered in front of a marine base in Baharai to show support for the SCAF.

Tens of thousands of protesters in Alexandria marked the first anniversary of the Jan 25 uprising as five marches met in front of Al-Qeddesine Church chanting against military rule. There were other demands chanted from different groups as well, such as a raise in the national minimum wage.
The killing of two Copts sparks a protest of 1,500.
Tens of thousands of protesters spanning all political factions marched Friday from Al-Qaed Ibrahim Mosque to the Central Military District chanting against the military rule.
Protesters in Alexandria threw tomatoes, one of them hitting an Egyptian official in the face at Hilary Clintons car
Angry workers storm Suez government buildings in frustration at lack of progress on long-standing grievances; security forces fire tear gas to disperse protesters

Hundreds protested at Alexandrias Corniche road in Al-Mansheya area asking for a policeman to be brought to justice for shooting at three people resulting in the death of a young man and the injury of others.
Angry Muslims attacked a church and Christian homes outside Cairo on Wednesday, sparking clashes that wounded 16 people
Police arrested 20 people for blocking a main road in Egypt in a protest over the alleged torture and death of a man
In Fayoum, the Brotherhood organized several protests attended by hundreds who condemned al-Assads regime
In Alexandria, hundreds of the Brotherhoods members and those who belong to the Islamist current protested in front of al-Qaed Ibrahim mosque in solidarity with the Syrian people.
Qaliubiya witnessed a number of protests to support the people Syria against the regime.
Damietta witnessed a number of protests to support the people Syria against the regime.
Menofeya witnessed a number of protests to support the people Syria against the regime.
Three arrested at student protest in Assiut
Sit-in protest continues for 20th day in Aswan demanding the resignation of Al-Sayed for his involvement in several corruption cases
Around 400 protesters marched from al-Qaed Ibrahim mosque in Alexandria to demand the trial of those responsible for the Mohamed Mahmoud Street and the Cabinet of Ministers crimes
Police shoot tear gas to disperse Supply Ministry and Interior Trade employees protesting the Qena officials lack of action on their long-held demands
Hundreds of people from the Al-Risa district of Al-Arish blocked the main road towards Rafah in North Sinai to protest the kidnapping of a 17 year old girl.
Doctors organised a short lived protest in the Al-Arish General Hospital in solidarity with the Doctors Syndicates decision to engage in a partial strike

A group of Bedouins from the Tarabin tribe blocked the international Sharm el-Sheikh road about three kilometres away from Ras Sedr protesting because a relative of theirs had been arrested and kept in custody

23 July Street, the main street in Al-Arish, was blocked in an attempt to put more pressure on the Ministry of Interior and the prosecution office after a man was shot dead by a policeman in North Sinai

About 40 youth from the Free Egyptians party, Egypt National party and the band Heart of Egypt formed a human chain in front of the Security Directorate in Menya, in response to an event cancelled intended to promote religious tolerance and celebrate national unity.
In Alexandria, hundreds demonstrated at the Qaed Ibrahim Mosque in solidarity with Gaza
Dozens of Muslim Brotherhood members demonstrated in Aswan city in solidarity with Gaza
In Qalyoubeia governorate, the Muslim Brotherhood together with Gamaa Islamiya called for demonstrations also attended by several other political groups in the governorates main squares
Dozens of Muslim Brotherhood members demonstrated in Aswans Kom Ombo in solidarity with Gaza
Hundreds demonstrated in Gharbeias Tanta city in a protest called by the FJP and the Salafist Calling in solidarity with Gaza
Tens of activists gathered at the Assiut governorate headquarters calling for the resignation of governor Yehia Keshk after a fatal bus accident killed 50 children
Protests also took place at the Assiut hospital over the Assiut fatal bus-train accident, broken up by police.
Protesters attacked the Islamists office in the city of Mahalla. 132 people injured.
Muslim Brotherhood HQ attacked by protesters
Thousands gathered in Qait Ibrahim square in Alexandria to protest against Mohamed Morsi
Hundreds demonstrated in Sharm el-Sheikh against Mohamed Morsi
27 were hurt in clashes in Port Said
Pro-Morsi protests were also staged in the city of Alexandria
Pro-Morsi protests were also staged in Assiut
Demonstrators also took to the streets in the Mediterranean city of Alexandria
Demonstrations in Sohag against Morsi
Protests in support of and against the constitutional declaration and draft of the constitution in Alexandria
Hundreds in Alexandria organized a march from al-Haqqaneya Court in al-Mansheya to Sidi Gaber Square
April 6 Movement organized a march from Roushdi area in Alexandria
The Constitution Party organized protest as well in Sidi Gaber.
Clashes erupted between supporters and opponents of President Mohamed Mursi in Kafr al-Sheikh
Al Wafd, plans a march to denounce an attack on their headquarters, allegedly by Islamist Salafist
Dozens of members of the Al-Ghul family, a sub-clan of the larger Al-Fuwakhiria tribe of North Sinai, protested outside Sinai University
Confrontations broke out between security forces and residents in Alexandria during an attempt to disperse a demonstration. Dozens injured
Around 100 protesters demonstrated Alexandrias al-Qaed Ibrahim Mosque denouncing the Muslim Brotherhoods rule and the newly-approved constitution.

Scuffles ensued on Tuesday between protesters and security forces in Alexandrias railway station after protesters cut off the railway line following the tragic Bardashin train accident that happened overnight. The security forces fired teargas to disperse the protesters following the injury of three central security officers during the scuffles.
In Suez, protesters have climbed the governorate building, raising the Suez flag and declaring the fall of the governor and the Morsy regime.
Thousands of people protested in Port Said on Saturday to demand separation from the state of Egypt. The protesters chanted for a Port Said state and against Al Ahly sports club and Ultras Ahlawy.

Hundreds of protesters have surrounded the Office of Al-Mahala City Council, declaring the illegitimacy of President Morsy, the independence of Al-Mahala from the current regime and the establishment of an executive council.

Hundreds of members of the hardcore soccer fans (Ultras White Knights) and other political forces staged mass protests on Tuesday in front of Alexandrias court complex in solidarity with those arrested in the background of Sundays clashes near the Criminal Court.

Thousands of citizens in Damietta on Friday participated in a number of marches from different mosques to al-Saa Square chanting slogans against President Mursi and the Muslim Brotherhood. The protesters denounced the sovereign Islamic bonds projects which the government plans to implement.
Hundreds of protesters who broke into Kafr al-Sheikhs city council on Friday demanded that the governor tenders his resignation, giving him a two-hour ultimatum to respond to their demand.

Thousands of protesters accompanied by Ultras Masrawy and Green Eagles (fans of al-Masry football club) on Friday participated in marches in Port Said on the second anniversary of the January 2011 uprising.

Around 3,000 protesters in Suez organised on Monday night a protest to march from al-Arbaeen Square to al-Geish Street to demand ending the 30-day curfew President Mohamed Mursi has imposed on three Suez Canal cities.

assoc_actor_1: Al-Wafd Party; Al-Dostour Party; Al-Karama Party; Socialist Revolutionaries; Sinai Freedom Movement
notes: Protesters gather in the North Sinai city of El Arish following Friday prayers. The demonstrators were chanting against President Mohamed Morsy.
Eight protesters (another report suggests over a dozen) were injured in clashes which erupted in Kafr Al-Sheikh, adding to a string of injuries in the governorate

In the governorate of Minya police thwarted an attempt to storm into the Muslim Brotherhoods headquarters, MENA reported. This came after clashes where police fired tear gas at protesters and protesters threw rocks at the police.

Hundreds in Alexandria gathered in front of the security directorate to protest against the treatment of Hamada Saber, the man who was caught on camera being beaten and dragged on the ground naked by police at the presidential palace on Friday.
The Kafr Al-Zayat Police Station in the governorate of Gharbeya was surrounded Saturday night until the early hours of Sunday by a group of youths from different political parties and movements.

Hundreds of police personnel in the Kafr Al-Sheikh governorate closed down the security directorate building and blocked traffic in front of it. They demanded retribution for police officers killed and demanded security procedures be reconsidered to allow officers be armed

In Alexandria, protesters marched from the Al-Qaed Ibrahim Mosque and gathered in front of the house of detained activist Hassan Mostafa in the Moharram Bey district calling for his release. Mostafa was taken into custody three weeks ago after he went to a prosecution office to file a complaint stating that activists were being detained without following the required legal procedures for the detention and without undertaking any investigations.
A small protest was also held in Al-Mamar Square of Ismailia, where protesters gathered after Friday prayers chanting slogans criticising the Muslim Brotherhood and Morsis rule in Egypt.

South Rafah residents held a poetry reading on Friday night in protest over Law 203, passed by Egypts Defense Minister last December, which prevents residents from owning or using land within five kilometers of Sinais eastern border with Israel.
Scores of members of revolutionary movements stage protest in Suez to demand realization of revolutions goals, release of detained revolutionaries, denounce attempts to intimidate opposition in Suez.

Scores of opposition activists demonstrate in Kafr al-Shaykh to demand realization of revolutions goals amid heavy security presence near state establishments. Demonstrators hurled stones at security forces guarding governors office, stormed foyer of local council building, security forces did not converge on scene to avoid escalation.
In Al-Minufiyah Governorate, opposition forces organised demonstrations to demand realization of the revolutions goal amid heavy security presence around state institutions.
Port Said channel correspondent reported small demonstrations, noted the death of a demonstrator who was hit by stray bullet near police station.

After days of protests and strikes conducted by police warrant officers, Minister of Interior Mohamed Ibrahim agreed to 19 of the protesters demands.The nationwide strike primarily demanded the repeal of the protest law passed by the Shura Council, Egypts upper house of parliament. The new protest law bans military and police personnel from engaging in any acts of protest, which includes strikes and sit-ins. The striking policemen also demanded the removal of the Minister of Interior from the cabinet. Dozens are participating in the strike in Assiut, Luxor, Aswan and North Sinai while hundreds did so in Monufia and Qalyubia. Several cities, including Mansoura and Beni Suef, saw policemen temporarily suspend their sit-in to give authorities time to respond to their demands.

Violent clashes broke out between protesters and security forces in Mahalla on Saturday after the death of a 14 year-old protester on Friday. Mahmoud Haroun, an activist and Mahalla resident, said that security forces from the nearby Mahalla Second Police Station became angry at the protesters chants and attempted to disperse them. Clashes broke out between both sides, leaving 36 injured,Haroun claimed that security forces used teargas and birdshot during the clashes, adding that most of the injuries were caused by birdshot.

Security source at Interior Ministry reports arresting 57 rioters in Cairo, Al-Gharbiyah, Alexandria governorates. Source says 30 arrested outside Al-Qubbah Palace, including one in possession of gun.
Mahalla also witnessed another round of protests, where protesters gathered in front of the city council building calling for civil disobedience.

Demonstrations in front of the courthouse in solidarity with Osama El-Refaey, member of the Al-Dostour Party, who was arrested last week in Beheira on charges of attempting to burn a government building, according to his lawyer, Mohammed Abouelela. Trial postponed.

Kafr El-Sheikh followed in the footsteps of the canal cities, with its first day of civil disobedience on Tuesday as protesters closed down the governorate building, Tax Authority, and the insurance directorate. A spokesperson for the 6 of April movement (the Ahmed Maher front) in Kafr El-Sheikh, affirmed the protesters demands and added that they initially took to the streets in solidarity with the residents of Port Said.
In Ismailia protesters gathered on Wednesday morning in front of the governorate building and the city council in solidarity with Port Said.

In Al-Ismailiyah Governorate, political activists continue to encourage civil disobedience near state establishments, schools to demand governments ouster, formation of national salvation government, political guarantees of fair parliamentary elections. Senior Army commander warns that locals would bear costs of any civil disobedience.
Hundreds of demonstrators took to the streets of Port Said on Friday to demand justice for protesters killed by Egyptian police, as a strike in the Suez Canal city entered its sixth day.

In the northern city of Kafr el-Sheikh, hundreds of quarry workers stormed the governorate headquarters to protest against working conditions and forced employees out of the building, chanting against governor Saad al-Husseini, a member of the Muslim Brotherhood.
In Giza, protesters gathered on Sunday night in front of the governorate building blocking Al-Haram Street and calling for civil disobedience.

For the third consecutive day, families of prisoners in Sinai blocked off the Al-Arish central Sinai road in southern al-Arish, preventing night shift workers from arriving at cement factories in the centre of the peninsula.

Port Saids continuing civil disobedience has entered its ninth day, with protesters continuing to block roads and shutting down government buildings.Three of the regions largest maritime shipping lines, CMA-CGM from France, COSCO from China, and MAERSK Line from Denmark, have redirected their cargoes from Port Said to the Israeli port of Eilat,

A protest also occurred in Matariya of Daqahleya when protesters surround the city council building, expressing solidarity with the cities joining in with the civil disobedience. No violence was reported following the protest.

Hundreds of protesters were marching towards Alexandrias local city council in the Kom El-Dekka district, where they planned to hold a protest, when several men attacked them with knives and fireworks, leaving some injured.
Protesters in Ismailia near the vital Suez Canal block the main road and try to block the railway, announcing they are joining the civil disobedience campaign seen in nearby cities.
Ittihad of Alexandria players have boycotted the clubs training session on Sunday to protest non-payment of their salaries.

Residents of the governorates of Qena, Aswan and Al-Sharqeya blocked railways and roads on Sunday morning in protest over the fuel shortage. In Aswan, drivers blocked Al-Sadat road after they were unable to fill their fuel tanks, reported state-owned Al-Ahram. Security officials were able to clear the blockage an hour later.

Residents of the governorates of Qena, Aswan and Al-Sharqeya blocked railways and roads on Sunday morning in protest over the fuel shortage. In Nag Hammadi, Qena, tens of taxi drivers and farmers blocked the railway from 7 am on Sunday, Governmental officials negotiated with the protesters and by noon had convinced them to resolve the blockage.

Hundreds of members of the hardcore soccer fans (Ultras Alexandria) besieged the headquarters of the Central Bank and cut off a main road in Alexandria. There were no acts of vandalism or any damages in the bank.The banks siege lasted for about 40 minutes, paralyzing the traffic in the area.

Tension between students and the administration at Misr International University (MIU) over road safety measures reached a peak on Monday.Protesting students gathered on Monday to condemn the referral of 12 students to investigation.
Acquaintances of a family killed by the police on Tuesday held a protest in Al-Shoon Square in Mahalla on Wednesday, calling for the punishment of police officers who killed the family.
Live two-way from Alexandria with Nile TV correspondent, who says police officers staging sit-in, raising slogans criticizing Interior Ministry policy in dealing with situation.
Central Security Forces (CSF) conscripts and police NCOs are holding a sit-in, demanding the removal of Minister of Interior Mohamed Ibrahim.

Protesters marched through Kafr El-Sheikh city in Kafr El-Sheikh governorate from the Rahma Mosque in the west and the sports stadium in the east, holding up loaves of bread signifying their dissatisfaction with the state of the economy.
Police officers shut down Muharam Beik Police Station, also demanding the removal of Ibrahim.

Egyptian police officers closed on Saturday all the police stations in Alexandria and reduced the presence of the Central Security Forces in front of Alexandrias Security Directorate, in an escalation of their protest against the policies of the Interior Ministry for not responding to their demands.
In Minya, police officers closed the gates of the security directorate.

In the city of Al-Arish, the Union of Unemployed Workers of Northern Sinai organised a protest on Sunday morning in front of the Public Governors Office, and demanded that job opportunities in Service Directorate positions or in the City Council be awarded to city residents.
Clashes also took place on Saturday, as protesters hurled rocks and Molotov cocktails at Mahalla second police station after their gathering at Al-Shoon square was attacked by armed men.
Protests and a sit-in in Belbeis called for the dismissal of Mayor Hassan Deba, a member of the Muslim Brotherhood.
Construction workers in Suez close Al-Sukhnah Port power station, hold sit-in there to protest hiring people from outside governorate at station.

Students of Misr International University (MIU) entered the second day of a sit-in at the university campus, calling for cancelling the expulsion of six students, better road safety, and freedom to protest.
Al-Ahram newspapers website reported that pro-army demonstrations were also held in Damietta in northern Egypt.
Masses of demonstrators marched in Port Said to protest against the rule of Mursi and the Brotherhood days after a court ruling that sentenced 21 to death in the 2012 Port Said football killing.
Oppositionists and unemployed youths in Suez organised a demonstration in al-Arbaeen Square to demand completing the revolutions objectives and providing job opportunities for youth.
Scores gathered at al-Shoun Square in Gharbiya to protest against the regime while others demonstrated in Tantas al-Shohadaa Square and chanted anti-Mursi slogans.
In addition to that, some journalists staged a protest objecting to being denied entry to the press conference.

Meanwhile, around 500 persons who belong to Islamist Parties staged protests in front of the stadium to protest the entry of anti-revolution personal into the stadium during the presidents press conference.
Mahalla microbus and taxi drivers blocked roadsand railways across the city on Sunday in protest to the ongoing diesel crisis in the city.
In an event in Beheira on Sunday, activists bore signs calling for the release of Mustafa and all other detainees who had been unjustly arrested.
Striking microbus drivers continued to block roads and ralways across Mahalla on Monday, in protest against the ongoing diesel crisis in the city.
Around 150 demonstrators gathered in Victoria, Alexandria, on Monday evening for a demonstration in solidarity with imprisoned activist Hassan Mustafa.
Railway traffic stops in Sidi Jabir, Alexandria, as citizens demonstrated to protest fuel shortage.

In Gharbeya, a protest was held on Wednesday against the attack on the office of Deputy Minister of Endowments Sabry Ebada; few were injured in the attack. The protesters stated that about 60 imams affiliated with the Brotherhood attacked Ebadas office for his stances against the Brotherhood.

A group of protesters gathered in front of the military prosecution building in Suez on Wednesday against the dispersal of unemployed demonstrators the evening before in Port Tawfiq. The protesters also demanded the release of six arrested protesters.Personnel from the Third Field Army removed barriers erected by protesters in order to allow trucks to enter Port Tawfiq on Tuesday night so that they could continue loading and unloading ships at the port.

Also on Thursday, in the town of Al-Wasta in the central governorate of Beni Suef, members of the Salafist Nour Party and El-Gamaa El-Salafaya (the Salafist Group) gathered in front of the police station to protest the alleged kidnapping of a 20-year-old Muslim woman who they claim was forced to convert to Christianity

Clashes have broken out between hundreds of locals outside a church in the city of Kom Ombo in Aswan on Thursday, as reports circulated that a 37-year-old Muslim woman had been kidnapped and forced to convert to Christianity. Teargas was being fired by security forces, and there were reports of clashes between Copts who were protecting the church and local Muslims who were throwing rocks.
On Friday night in Alexandria, well-known activist Mahienour Al-Masry, together with several others, was arrested after they staged a protest in support for those detained in the latest clashes.

In Al-Gharbiyah Governorate, scores of youth, who belong to revolutionary movements, organize demonstrations in Al-Mahallah to protest arrest of activists during Al-Muqattam events. Demonstrators demanded achieving revolution goals, investigation into Al-Muqattam events.
In Suez, activists stage demonstration in Al-Arbain Square to demand sacking general prosecutor.

Following the arrest of protesters at Al-Raml on Friday night, lawyers in solidarity with the detainees have decided to carry-out a sit in at Al-Raml Prosecution.According to Ahmed El Mansi, all protesters were beaten during the protest and 13 were arrested.
Air traffic completely stopped on Sunday in Alexandrias Borg Al-Arab International Airport, following protests by hundreds of its guards demanding better working conditions and improved salaries.
A family of Bedouins renewed their sit-in on Sunday morning in front of the North Sinai Public Office, demanding that authorities reinstate their Egyptian citizenship.

Port Said residents protested on Sunday against statements made by the head of the revolutionary victims fund Khaled Badawy, who said that those who died during the latest Port Said clashes are not on the funds list of victims of the revolution.

In Sundays protest at Misr International University, students demanded a pedestrians bridge outside the university gate to prevent accidents. Protesting students marched to University Deputy Chariman, Hassans office to put forward their demand. They claim to have been stopped by the security personnel.

Thousands of demonstrators on Friday participated in marches in Cairo, Alexandria and Luxor in support of the independence of al-Azhar and its Grand Imam Sheikh Ahmed al-Tayeb. In Luxor, hometown of al-Tayeb, demonstrators gathered at Abul Hoggag Square behind Luxor Temple to express their support for al-Tayeb.

A small number of protesters gathered outside the court in Alexandria, in a show of solidarity with workers at the Alexandria Container Holding company, whose trial was postponed until 16 June.

Health, Population Ministry announces one killed, 14 injured in clashes outside Mary Gerges Church in Al-Khusus, Al-Qalyubiyah governorate, north of Cairo. State of cautious calm reported in Al-Khusus, with heavy security forces deployed in city.
Strike:Around 40 port guards also went on strike in the Nuweiba port, protesting a decision to move them to a different port.
Dozens of Salafis protested on Tuesday in the Al-Hadra district of Alexandria against the Shia doctrine.

Alexandria police chief, security leadership negotiate with protestors in Burj al-Arab airport to convince them to end protest. Officers who tried to return airport functions back to normal were earlier expelled by protestors.

Scores of supply department inspectors in Al-Buhayrah Governorate continue to protest for third day, demanding dismissal of Supply, Internal Trade Ministrys undersecretary, department heads following death of two inspectors. Hundreds protested outside supply department headquarters in Damanhur.

Demonstrators on Friday staged protests at Kasr ElDobara Church in Cairo and St. Mary Church in Imbaba in solidarity with Copts and in support of national unity. In Imbaba, members of the April 6 Youth movement organised a march at St. Mary Church which was torched by Salafi protesters in 2011.

Clashes broke out on Friday afternoon between supporters and opponents of the Muslim Brotherhood in Alexandrias Victor Emmanuel Square as both parties exchanged stones. As anti-brotherhood demonstrators arrived in Victor Emmanuel Square, they were met by Muslim Brotherhood supporters hurling stones and empty bottles at them .

Dozens of low-ranking policeman at the Borg Al-Arab International Airport staged protests against suspending ten of their colleagues and issuing a decision to transfer them to the Marsa Matrouh Airport on Sunday. The protesters also demanded the return of police officers to their work after the police officers had handed over a mass resignation to the interior minister over allegedly being insulted in a previous confrontation with the low-ranking security staff.

The stagecoach driver strike taking place in front of the Karnak temple in Luxor has officially ended after officers heard the demands of those striking and promised to find ways to address their problems, announced Luxors Tourist Police. The strike started early on Sunday morning when drivers blocked the roads leading to the temple, protesting what they described as unfair practices by tourist companies favouring some drivers over others.

Protesters gathered outside the Saudi embassy in Giza on Wednesday to mark one year since the arrest of Egyptian lawyer Ahmed Al-Gizawy by Saudi security forces. Al-Gizawy was sentenced to five years in prison and 300 lashes by the General Court of Jeddah in Saudi Arabia in January. He was accused of drug trafficking.
Hundreds of the Muslim Brotherhood members and supporters had gathered at Asr al-Islam Mosque in Al-exandrias Sidi Gaber to take part in mass demonstrations the brotherhood had called for earlier.

Hundreds of activists and demonstrators on Friday marched in Alexandria from al-Qaed Ibrahim Mosque to the Judges Club to denounce what they called the attempt to brotherhoodise the judiciary and the state. They also demanded holding those responsible for assaulting peaceful demonstrators and kidnapping activists accountable.The demonstrators chanted slogans against the Muslim Brotherhood and President Mohamed Mursi.

In Gharbiya, thousands rallied in Tantas al-Sayed al-Badawi Square to take part in a demonstration calling for supporting the judiciary, the army, the police and al-Azhar. The demonstrators chanted pro-army and anti-Brotherhood slogans and called on the army to take to the streets.

Members of the April 6 Youth Movement staged a protest commemorating the death of Khaled Saied.

Members of 6 April Youth Movement (Ahmed Maher front) gathered in front of Abdeen Court on Saturday, to protest the arrest and detainment of their members, and demanding investigations into the death of Gaber 'Jika' Salah.

Dozens of students gathered outside the High Court building on Tuesday afternoon, calling for the release of student Mahmoud Safwat and others who were detained last weekend. The students marched from Cairo University and Helwan University to the High Court building in Downtown Cairo chanting against the Ministry of the Interior and the Muslim Brotherhood.Friends claim Safwat was arrested on Friday morning and was one of 22 people for whom Prosecutor General Talaat Abdallah issued an arrest warrant for alleged connections to the Black Bloc.
The 6 April Youth Movement (Ahmed Maher Front) conducted a march in Downtown Cairo, vowing an escalation of peaceful protests, before the larger marches took place.

Dozens of Egyptians protested, both Muslims and Christians, in Cairo to demand the introduction of civil marriages alongside existing religious marriages, to make it easier to get divorced and remarry.

A protest was held in front of the prosecutor generals office on Saturday, calling for finding Mohamed El-Sawy, a journalist at the Masrawy news outlet, who was reportedly kidnapped on Wednesday and punishing his kidnappers.

A small protest took place in front of the High Court building in Cairo on Tuesday, organised by members of Tawfik Okashas Al-Faraeen television channel and supporters from his hometown. The protesters said they want the Prosecutor General to rescind the six month sentence handed to Okasha in a case regarding an unpaid divorce settlement between him and his ex-wife Reda Al-Qardawy, but they also support Okasha more generally in all his legal cases.Protesters prevented the Daily News Egypt from covering the event, alleging the journalist worked for 'foreign hands'. Last year Tawfik Okasha was accused of insulting the president on 30 separate charges.

Tens of women gathered on Wednesday in front of the Prosecutor Generals office, in the High Court complex, protesting against statements insulting women made by several high profile figures recently.Protesters brandished signs expressing their anger over government policies and stating their demands. They criticised the Shura Council for the recent statements it has made about women, blaming women protesters for sexual harassment and claiming that women should not participate in protests.Shahenda Maqlad, a prominent female activist and the secretary general of the Farmers Union, attended the protest. Maqlad came to prominence after a Morsi supporter was photographed forcibly shutting her mouth during protests at the Itihadiya presidential palace two months ago.

In Sundays protest at Misr International University, students demanded a pedestrians bridge outside the university gate to prevent accidents. Photojournalists protested in front of the Shura Council on Tuesday in reaction to increasing numbers of attacks against media workers in Egypt. Dozens of protesters held a silent demonstration on Sunday in front of the Forensic Medical Authority in Cairo, to reject reports issued by the authority for those who died during several incidents since the 2011 Revolution. Protesters claimed the authority is corrupt and partial, accusing it of forging reports to hide the truth behind the cause of death for many revolutionary figures. Several revolutionary groups participated in the demonstration, including NWT, the Our Name is Mohamed El-Gendy campaign, Essam Atta group, and the Popular Current.NWT held a press conference on Saturday, where it stated there were 800 detention and 70 death cases occurred in Egypt during the period from 25 January to 11 February 2013. On 09 September 2011, thousands of protesters gathered in front of the High Court in Cairo to denounce corruption and reject military tribunals for the prosecution of civilians. .

On 7 May 2011, dozens of Islamist lawyers protested in front of the Egyptian Lawyers Association in Cairo, to denounce the killing of Osama bin Laden by the Americans, calling on the international community and human rights organizations to investigate the killing, which they consider as a war crime.

assoc_actor_1: RYA: Revolutionary Youth Alliance; Muslim Brotherhood; Wafd Party
notes: On 02 August 2011, a civilian protest took place in front of the Syrian embassy in Cairo during which protesters denounced the Asad regime violence against civilians and demanded the expulsion of the Syrian ambassador. The protest was attended by 150 people including members of the Muslim Brotherhood, the Wafd Party, and the Revolutionary Youth Alliance.

On 22 May 2011, dozens of employees of Amoncito Textile Company held a protest in front of Egypt Bank headquarters, to demand the disbursement of 41 million of their remaining dues after being on the early pension, and the decision to liquidate the company.

On 23 June 2011, dozens of students from the literary section of the General high school, gathered in front of the Ministry of Education in Cairo, to express their anger over the difficulty of the geology exam questions.

On 23 May 2011, the family of the police secretary, Mohamed Abdel Moneim, famously referred to as Mohammed al-Sunni, whose papers were sent to the Grand Mufti to give his legal opinion on his execution, organized a protest in front of the Ministry of Justice against the decision to refer him in his absence.

On 29 June 2011, a number of demonstrators marched to the headquarters of the Ministry of the Interior in Cairo, and surrounded the building, causing confrontations with security forces. A number of them threw bricks at the building, and the security forces responded by throwing tear gas at them, which resulted in injuries. Demonstrators also set a number of tires and wood on fire in the street near the ministry. A number of residents living in the area joined the security forces and participated in the clash against the demonstrators.

On 14 May 2011, more than 70 students from technical institutes and diplomas, held a sit-in the office of Dr. Amr Ezzat Salama, Minister of Higher Education, Scientific Research and Technology, at he ministry of Higher Education, after the siege of the office from 3 to 7:30. Police forces intervened to calm the situation.

On 26 June 2011, 40 doctors specialized in Natural Medicine gathered inside the Ministry of Health in Cairo, to oppose the separation of doctors from the specialized ones technically and administratively, and to demand the cancellation of the decision issued by the Minister of Health, which requires the presence of specialized doctors in Natural Medicine clinics.

On 5 May 2011, demonstrators from the families of criminal prisoners blocked Magles Al Shab street in Cairo, after demonstrations for 3 days without reaching a solution with any of the officials. They were also joined by dozens of temporary workers in Damanhour General Hospital, sitting on the ground after the demonstration for two hours to demand their establishment in their jobs. The road cut continued for more than an hour, leading to some verbal clashes between car drivers and demonstrators.

On 3 May 2011, dozens of workers at the Center of Technical Support in Alexandria, which is under the Ministry of Health and Population, gathered in front of the Cairo headquarters of the ministry, to demand the suspension of the decision to annex the headquarters of the Center and merge it to Alexandria governorate, and to maintian its dependence on the Ministry of Health.

On 2 May 2011, dozens of teachers gathered in front of the Ministry of Education to demand a solution to the financial crisis, and find an alternative method of evaluating teachers to replace the tests of staff. There were confrontations between the demonstrators and a number of security personnel of the Ministry of Interior. Demonstrators crossed into the building, after opening the main gate.

On 3 May 2011, dozens of Egyptian mothers married to Palestinians and their children demonstrated in front of the Ministry of the Interior to demand approval of the decision to grant Egyptian citizenship to their children.

On 2 May 2011, a number of relatives of Egyptian detainees in Libya broke into the headquarters of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, to demonstrate against what they called 'the failure of the ministry to intervene to release their relatives,' while ministry officials called for the armed forces to remove the people from within the ministry.

On 28 June 2011, the security-excluded imams moved their sit-in, which entered its seventh day, from the sidewalk of Al Qasr al Ayni Street, to the Council of Ministers to ask Dr. Essam Sharaf to re-appoint them.

On 30 November 2011, a number or representatives of candidates who ran in the elections that took place the day before staged a sit-in in front of the electoral committee's headquarters in city of Shubra Al-Khayma to protest against the cancellation of the counting of votes.

A group of Muslims go on a rampage when rumor spread a Copt had a 'intimate picture' of a Muslim woman on his cell phone. Many homes and shot owned by Copts were burned. Eight families were forcibly evicted by the attackers.

Dozens of Sudanese refugees in Cairo held a sit-in in front of the United Nations Commission for Refugees [UNHCR] which is located in Six October town in Giza province, protesting the very -worsening humanitarian situations they suffer in Egypt.
Marches began after Friday prayers departing from a total of 28 locations across Cairo to converge on the ongoing sit-ins at Rabaa Al-Adaweya in Nasr City and Nahda Square in Giza.

Dozens of Ethiopian refugees protested outside the Egypt office of UN refugee agency (UNHCR) in Cairos 6 October City to demand protection from what they describe as increasingly frequent xenophobic attacks by Egyptians
Two marches will start at 6 October Al-Hossari and Ragheb mosques
The Anti-Coup Coalition announced further marches taking place on Sunday from a number of locations around Cairo.
Islamists rallied outside Media City to show support for Morsi
6 October City witnessed clashes between pro- and anti-Morsy protesters who were throwing stones at each other. The security forces separated the sides
Hundreds of protesters surrounded the main offices of the state security agency in 6th of October City to demand an end to alleged burning of evidence of human rights abuses.
Supporters of Morsi held a march to the Egyptian Media Production City.

Several thousands of Morsis backers gathered outside the Egyptian Media Production City. They carried the Egyptian flags and Morsis pictures, chanting slogans against the 'corrupted' media via speakers
On 21 June 2011, administrators and workers at the control of the general high school exams in 6th October City, stopped working and held a protest against not being appointd so far.
Hundreds of revolutionary activists and representatives of national powers marched from Port Fouad ferries areas in a demonstration demanding the presidency for Hamdeen Sabahi.
In Suez, around 200 members of Suez Youth Bloc and April 6 movement in addition to some Brotherhood members and Salafists organised a protest
Protesting Suez Arsenal workers shut off electricity to the city. Suez Arsenal Company employees shut down the electricity to Port Tawfik district in Suez and lock the gates of company premises.
Opposition forces have called for marches after mid-day prayers from the Dawahy neighbourhood, al-Zohour neighbourhood, the new Fish Market and Ber Fouad. Three protesters injured.
Opposition forces have called for marches after mid-day prayers from the Dawahy neighbourhood, al-Zohour neighbourhood, the new Fish Market and Ber Fouad. Three protesters injured.
A sit-in was started in the Port Tawfiq area of Suez as part of ongoing demonstrations across the country over corruption and police brutality.

notes: On 02 August 2011, a demonstration took place in Al-Arbaeen Square in Suez to denounce the violent dispersion of the Tahrir Square sit-in by the military police. The protest was attended by 200 people including members of Suez Youth Bloc and April 6 Youth Movement.
On 28 June 2013, unidentified assailants threw a small explosive device at anti-regime demonstrators who were marching in Port Said (Al-Munakh, Port Said). A journalist covering the event was killed.
On 8 April 2011, 2 protesters were killed and 18 wounded when the army tried to disperse them with force in Cairo (Cairo, Cairo).

Early on April 9 (around 2-5am), hundreds of soldiers backed by armored vehicles used batons, rubber bullets and live ammunition to break up a sit-in opposing SCAF's rule, wounding at least 71 protesters and killing two (according to medical sources reported on by the AFP). 42 people were also arrested.
Christians gathered in front of the state TV and radio building to protest religious violence after a recent attack of two churches that resulted in death and injury.

Protesters gather to press for government reforms. The military used excessive force and carried out arbitrary mass arrests to disperse demonstrations and sit-ins - beating and tasering those arrested.

Protests over the Israeli air forces killing of 5 Egyptian police in an airstrike were held in front of the cabinet building in downtown Cairo. Protestors were joined by tens of Cairo University students. Protestors demanded the expulsion of the Israeli ambassador.

Police repulsed supporters of the former president Mubarak who attempted to storm the courthouse at Cairo's police academy where he was on trial. Some of the demonstrators were detained. The pro-Mubarak supporters also clashed with anti-Mubarak supporters inside and outside the courthouse. 11 people were injured in the clashes. 22 people were arrested, including a 1 member of April 6 Youth Movement.

Thousands of protesters, mainly members of April 6 Youth Movement and Al-Wasat Party, begin returning to Tahir Square to 'reclaim the revolution' amid anger over the military rulers handling of the transition.
On 7 November 2011, hundreds of Egyptians marched from downtown Cairo to Torah prison in solidarity with an activist currently being investigated by the military courts.

On 13 November 2011, hundreds of youths protested against military trials for civilians in downtown Cairo. They marched and then rallied in Tahrir Square, calling for compensation from the Mubarak era and the end to corruption.

On 15 November 2011, more than 50 family members of those martyred or injured during 'the revolution' began a sit-in in Tahrir Square and announced their boycott of the parliamentary elections. They set up tents and vowed to continue their protest until the demands of the revolution are implemented. They also set up roadblocks on the square and prevented cars from passing through.

On 16 November 2011, hundreds of ETUF workers began sit-in in front of the ETUF offices on Galaa Street in downtown Cairo to protest against the labour minister who they say is attempting to destroy the country's burgeoning labour movement.

On 20 November 2011, thousands of rioters clashed with hundreds of soldiers and police in Tahrir Square; 11 are reported dead from clashing while over 700 were reported wounded. 40 police officers were wounded and 18 of the protesters were arrested. The police were trying to evict demonstrators who had camped out in the Square. Clashes between rioters and security forces were also reported at the Interior Ministry which is just a few blocks away from Tahrir Square. The Shura Council building was set on fire by a tear gas bomb.

On 25 November 2011, thousands of protesters marched in Tahrir Square demanded the resignation of the military leaders responsible for violence in recent days. Protesters disarmed a group of thugs who arrived seeking to cause trouble. The protesters also began a multi-day sit in.
On 25 November 2011, thousands of protesters gathered in Abbasiya Square in Cairo to show solidarity with the SCAF, calling themselves the 'Million Man March of the Silent Majority'.
On 2 December 2011, military junta supporters assemble in the Al Abbasiyah area of Cairo to show support of the military rule.

On 2 December 2011, thousands of protesters marched in Tahrir Square demanding the end of military trials for civilians, speedy trials for those responsible for the death of protesters, and the immediate handover of power to a civilian authority.

On 17 December 2011, security forces and police continued to clash with demonstrators from the day before in the area of Tahrir Square, resulting in a number of casualties. A library housing many irreplaceable books and documents burned in the violence with rioters breaking through police barriers and the army firing tear gas and rubber bullets. As many as 157 people were detained over the course of the 2 days of clashes.

On 19 December 2011, battles with police and military continued, 3 more reported dead from clashes. Live rounds were reported fired by security forces reported while rioters threw Molotov cocktails. 17 people were killed in total between Dec.16-19, including some who later died of their injuries (extra fatalities coded on Dec.18) while 12 were wounded over the two day clash.
On 20 December 2011, several hundred protesters gathered outside the Egyptian Supreme Court to show solidarity with the SCAF.
On 20 December 2011, thousands of Egyptian women marched in downtown Cairo against excessive force and sexual violence used by the Egyptian army against female protesters.
A protest against Egyptian President and the Muslim Brotherhood continued in Cairo. In a separate incident an Egyptian man set fire to himself in protest about losing his job.

Scores of protesters on Friday chanted slogans in Cairos Tahrir Square calling for the intervention of the Egyptian army, an Aswat Masriya witness reported. Hundreds gathered in the square to protest against the rule of President Mohamed Mursi and the Muslim Brotherhood.

Protesters on Sunday blocked the doors to Cairos main administrative building as part of a growing campaign of civil disobedience around the country against Islamist President Mohamed Morsi.A group of protesters closed the doors of the Mugamma, a massive labyrinth of bureaucratic offices on the edge of Tahrir Square, leaving only a side exit for employees to leave, employees told AFP

Coptic Christians engaged in a sit-in protest in front of the state TV and radio building have extended their demonstration following clashes outside a church in Ein Shams and the delay of a permit for the opening of a church in Minya.

The group chanted 'down with the military' and called on the ruling generals to 'return to the barracks.' A group of pro-Mubarak supporters ride camels and horses into a crowd of protesters, but soon are surrounded, dragged from their animal and beaten by protesters. One of the captured attackers was identified as a State security officer.

Thousands of protesters tried to march to the Defense Ministry of Egypt when they were attacked by groups of men wielding knives, sticks, stones and molotov cocktails. At many as 150 people were reported injured in the events. Military forces also fired into the air and used barbed wire blockades to block the protesters march; the attackers seem to have been supporters of the military.

At least 817 people were killed during the security operation to clear out the Muslim Brotherhoods sit-ins at Cairos Rabaa al-Adaweya according to HRW. Armored personnel carriers (APCs), bulldozers, ground forces, and snipers, police and army personnel attacked the makeshift protest encampment. Security forces opened fire on unarmed protesters using live ammunition, with hundreds killed by live bullets to their heads, necks, and chests. Snipers and gunmen fired into large crowds of protesters.Security forces detained over 800 protesters in the break up of the Rab'aa square protest encampment.
